source_type_legend: Each threshold carries a source_type field: 'statutory' = law/regulation, 'published_research' = named external study with year, 'internal_calibration' = consulting engagement data with no single external source.

peer_percentile_include.rationale: Flags categories where actual spend sits above the peer median. P50 is the minimum bar; P75-P90 and P90+ indicate increasingly severe overspend relative to peer group.
peer_percentile_include.source: Internal platform design — consistent with Hackett Group World-Class benchmark methodology which uses P25 as the top-quartile target and P75 as the underperformance flag.
peer_percentile_include.disclaimer: Band boundaries are internal design choices; the Hackett Group methodology referenced is directional, not directly quoted.

supplier_fragmentation_hhi_max.citation: DOJ/FTC Horizontal Merger Guidelines (2010) — Section 5.3: markets with HHI < 1500 (= 0.15 in 0-1 scale) are unconcentrated
supplier_fragmentation_hhi_max.rationale: An HHI below 0.15 within a spend category (across active suppliers) indicates fragmentation. HHI of 0 = perfectly fragmented; HHI of 1 = single supplier. Threshold of 0.15 set conservatively to catch meaningful fragmentation without over-triggering on categories with naturally distributed spend.
supplier_fragmentation_hhi_max.adaptation_note: DOJ/FTC uses HHI at market level for merger analysis. Applied here to supplier concentration within a single buyer's spend category — methodologically analogous but not the original intended use.

supplier_fragmentation_min_suppliers.rationale: Minimum number of active suppliers in a category before HHI fragmentation flag is meaningful. Fewer than 5 suppliers means HHI may reflect intentional dual-sourcing rather than unmanaged proliferation.
supplier_fragmentation_min_suppliers.source: Internal heuristic based on procurement best practice; consistent with Hackett Group procurement diagnostic thresholds.
supplier_fragmentation_min_suppliers.disclaimer: No specific Hackett Group publication sources this exact threshold. It is an internal judgment call.

maverick_spend_ratio_min.rationale: Spend through non-preferred channels (outside approved vendors or PO process) exceeding 20% of category spend indicates a compliance problem. CAPS Research data shows average managed spend is 85% of total (implying ~15% unmanaged); best-in-class is 91% managed (<9% maverick).
maverick_spend_ratio_min.source: CAPS Research (Institute for Supply Management) — capsresearch.org — benchmarks show average 85% spend under management, implying ~15% unmanaged spend. Hackett Group world-class AP threshold is <5% maverick spend. 20% threshold is an internal calibration at the median-to-poor boundary.
maverick_spend_ratio_min.disclaimer: The 20% threshold is an internal platform design choice. CAPS Research and Hackett are directional references, not the source of this specific threshold.

cost_per_transaction_max.rationale: Cost per invoice processed above ₹1,000 (or $12 USD) signals manual-intensive AP operations. Benchmark for automated AP is ₹100-300 ($1.2-3.6) per invoice; semi-automated ₹300-600 ($3.6-7.2); fully manual ₹600-1,500+ ($7.2-18+).
cost_per_transaction_max.source: Ardent Partners AP Metrics That Matter 2024 (ardentpartners.com) reports global cost-per-invoice benchmarks. India-market ₹ values are internal calibrations at ~83x USD/INR FX. Global automated benchmark: ~$3-7 USD per invoice; manual: ~$10-25 USD.
cost_per_transaction_max.disclaimer: The ₹1,000 / $12 thresholds are internal calibrations. Ardent Partners reports in USD; the INR equivalent is approximate and should be updated with current FX rates.

supplier_consolidation.rationale: 20% of fragmented category spend is typically addressable through consolidation in the first programme cycle — reflects that not all suppliers can be exited immediately due to contractual lock-ins and transition risk.
supplier_consolidation.source: Internal platform calibration based on consulting engagement outcomes. Directionally consistent with Hackett Group procurement value realisation benchmarks (not directly quoted from a specific study).
supplier_consolidation.disclaimer: Internal estimate. No single published source states 20% as a standard addressability rate for supplier consolidation.

maverick_compliance.rationale: 10% of total category spend is the conservative addressable quantum for maverick compliance programmes — reflects partial compliance uplift achievable without full P2P transformation.
maverick_compliance.source: Internal calibration. Hackett Group directionally references 3-8% of addressable spend as typical maverick reduction yield from a compliance programme alone; 10% is the internal platform upper-bound for first-cycle addressability.
maverick_compliance.disclaimer: Internal estimate. The 10% figure is not directly quoted from any Hackett Group publication.

demand_management.rationale: 8% addressability for demand management reflects discretionary spend reduction achievable through policy tightening and approval controls, without structural changes to operations.
demand_management.source: Internal calibration from India enterprise cost reduction programmes. McKinsey zero-based budgeting reports cite 10-25% reduction across cost categories when ZBB is applied comprehensively (not specifically discretionary-only). The 8% platform default is a conservative India-enterprise calibration for policy-level changes only.
demand_management.disclaimer: 8% is an internal platform default. McKinsey's 10-25% ZBB figure covers broader cost categories; the specific 8% for demand management is an internal calibration.

baseline_optimization.rationale: 5% floor applied when no specific root cause is identified — represents the minimum savings achievable through basic vendor negotiations and process clean-up in any addressable category.
baseline_optimization.source: Internal conservative floor. KPMG and Deloitte public cost optimisation frameworks directionally suggest 3-8% baseline savings in mature procurement organisations; 5% is the internal platform midpoint for this range.
baseline_optimization.disclaimer: Internal estimate. No KPMG or Deloitte publication specifically sources a 5% baseline floor figure.
